CodeIssue.Title: Public Class Not Declared as Final
CodeIssue.Description: The class is not declared as final as per OWASP recommendations. It is considered best practice to make classes final where possible and practical (i.e. It has no classes which inherit from it). Non-Final classes can allow an attacker to extend a class in a malicious manner. Manually inspect the code to determine whether or not it is practical to make this class final.

CodeIssue.Title: Public Class Not Declared as Final
CodeIssue.Description: The class is not declared as final as per OWASP recommendations. It is considered best practice to make classes final where possible and practical (i.e. It has no classes which inherit from it). Non-Final classes can allow an attacker to extend a class in a malicious manner. Manually inspect the code to determine whether or not it is practical to make this class final.

CodeIssue.Title: java.io.File
CodeIssue.Description: This functionality acts as an entry point for external data and the code should be manually checked to ensure the data obtained is correctly validated and/or sanitised. Additionally, carefull checks/sanitisation should be applied in any situation where the user may be able to control or affect the filename.

CodeIssue.Title: FileInputStream
CodeIssue.Description: This function acts as an entry point for external data and the code should be manually checked to ensure the data obtained is correctly validated and/or sanitised. Additionally, carefull checks/sanitisation should be applied in any situation where the user may be able to control or affect the filename.

CodeIssue.Title: Operation on Primitive Data Type
CodeIssue.Description: The code appears to be carrying out a mathematical operation on a primitive data type. In some circumstances this can result in an overflow and unexpected behaviour. Check the code manually to determine the risk.

CodeIssue.Title: Operation on Primitive Data Type
CodeIssue.Description: The code appears to be carrying out a mathematical operation on a primitive data type. In some circumstances this can result in an overflow and unexpected behaviour. Check the code manually to determine the risk.

CodeIssue.Title: Operation on Primitive Data Type
CodeIssue.Description: The code appears to be carrying out a mathematical operation on a primitive data type. In some circumstances this can result in an overflow and unexpected behaviour. Check the code manually to determine the risk.

CodeIssue.Title: getParameter
CodeIssue.Description: This function extracts data directly from the HTTP request. Manually check the code to ensure the data obtained is correctly validated and/or sanitised.

CodeIssue.Title: Failure To Release Resources In All Cases
CodeIssue.Description: There appears to be no 'finally' block to release resources if an exception occurs, potentially resulting in DoS conditions from excessive resource consumption.

CodeIssue.Title: Failure To Release Resources In All Cases
CodeIssue.Description: There appears to be no release of resources in the 'finally' block, potentially resulting in DoS conditions from excessive resource consumption.
